generated_by: manual G10-2 evidence family schema note
purpose: define coarse-grained evidence families used for admission, suppression, and budget decisions in G10 evidence-budgeted bounded planning

return_continuation.role: graph-side or promoted fallback candidate that closes return-oriented continuity

alias_continuation.role: graph-side candidate that closes alias/value/result continuity

weak_require_relative_continuation.role: weak Ruby continuation supported mainly by require_relative provenance rather than strong semantic continuity

module_companion_fallback.role: bounded Ruby fallback candidate admitted by companion/runtime provenance

return_semantic_continuity.role: direct semantic continuity for return-oriented completion

alias_semantic_continuity.role: direct semantic continuity for alias/value/result completion

fallback_promoted_semantic_continuity.role: semantic continuity discovered on a fallback candidate after bounded admission

semantic_provenance.role: local DFG, propagation, or stronger semantic aggregation support

call_position_strength.role: late or side-max callsite positional strength

positive_lexical_hint.role: weak positive lexical or structural hint that must not justify admission alone

require_relative_provenance.role: bounded require_relative load or structural provenance

dynamic_target_provenance.role: literal dynamic dispatch target family used to narrow fallback materialization

companion_path_provenance.role: companion/module path provenance used for bounded fallback admission

helper_noise_suppressor.role: suppresses helper/debug/tmp/noisy-return candidates before or during compare

fallback_only_suppressor.role: marks candidates that only have fallback provenance and lack meaningful semantic continuity

weaker_certainty_suppressor.role: marks weaker certainty against a stronger competitor

late_call_only_suppressor.role: marks candidates whose main strength is positional lateness without stronger continuity

profile_shape.name: CandidateAdmissionProfile
rules: family arrays are deduplicated and emitted in lexical order
support families alone do not justify admission
fallback families justify bounded fallback admission but do not replace semantic-ready primary by default
negative families stay explicit and are not inferred only by absence

semantic_ready: requires at least one primary family
must not trip a hard suppress gate
preferred admission class for graph-first candidates
fallback_ready: requires fallback family contract to be satisfied
used for weak require_relative and narrow fallback candidates before semantic promotion
structural_only: has support and/or partial fallback provenance but no meaningful primary family
should usually not enter the final compare pool unchanged
suppressed_before_admit: used when negative families dominate or a stronger representative already exists
must remain explainable in slice summary or witness surfaces

hard_suppress_gate: same-family weaker sibling that does not update representative coverage
representative_shadowing: same representative key but weaker profile
same candidate family and no new family coverage

same_path_duplicate_suppression.rule: keep one representative per path and merge reasons without spending extra budget unless family coverage improves

cross_family_per_seed_budget.rule: apply after family representatives are chosen, not before

family_budget_exhausted_when: a strongest representative for the candidate family already exists
the new candidate does not update representative quality or family coverage
keeping the new candidate would only duplicate same-family variation

stop_rules.family_representatives_satisfied: stop when the relevant family representatives are already present and only weaker siblings or duplicates remain
stop_rules.only_suppressing_leftovers: stop when remaining candidates are composed mainly of helper_noise, fallback_only, or late_call_only suppressors
stop_rules.fallback_provenance_exhausted: stop Ruby fallback widening when require_relative, dynamic_target, or companion provenance contracts can no longer be satisfied

g10_3: apply hard suppress gate before final compare pool admission
g10_4: use return_continuation and alias_continuation family representatives to suppress weaker Rust siblings
g10_5: apply require_relative, dynamic_target, and companion provenance contracts to Ruby fallback admission
g10_6: render dropped-before-admit and family-budget reasons using family labels
